scope.summary:
Goal: Implement an LSP (Language Server Protocol) client for the native code editor that provides: completion (triggered on `.`, `:`, `_` or Ctrl+Space), hover documentation (on cursor dwell), go-to-definition, find-references, and diagnostics (pushing results to the gutter from MT-007 via `push_diagnostics`). Additionally, bind the existing Handshake backend knowledge/code-nav APIs (`/knowledge/code/symbols`, `/knowledge/code/symbols/{id}`, `/knowledge/code/symbols/{id}/references`, `/knowledge/code/files/{path}/lens`) as a fallback intelligence source when no language server is attached. Port the `CodeSymbolPanel` component and `code_intelligence.ts` logic.

Expected diff shape: new module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/lsp_client.rs` (LSP JSON-RPC client over stdio to a language server process), new module `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/code_nav.rs` (Handshake backend code-nav API calls via the existing HTTP client), edits to `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` (wiring completion and hover popups into the editor panel), and `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/editor_view.rs` (completion popup widget and hover tooltip widget).

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/lsp_client.rs` — `LspClient { process: Option<Child>, sender: mpsc::Sender<LspRequest>, receiver: mpsc::Receiver<LspResponse> }`. Uses `tower-lsp` client-side or raw JSON-RPC over stdio. Implements: `initialize(root_uri)`, `did_open(uri, language_id, text)`, `did_change(uri, version, changes)`, `completion(uri, position) -> Vec<CompletionItem>`, `hover(uri, position) -> Option<HoverResult>`, `goto_definition(uri, position) -> Option<Location>`, `references(uri, position) -> Vec<Location>`, `publish_diagnostics callback -> Vec<LspDiagnostic>`. All requests are async; results are returned via a `tokio::sync::oneshot` channel. The LSP process is launched lazily on first `did_open`. If no language server is configured or the process fails to start, all methods return empty/None gracefully.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/code_nav.rs` — `CodeNavClient` that wraps the Handshake backend HTTP calls. Ports the logic from `app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.ts`. Methods: `lookup_symbols(workspace_id: &str, prefix: &str, limit: usize) -> Vec<CodeSymbolNavProjection>`, `get_symbol(entity_id: &str) -> Option<CodeSymbolResponse>`, `get_references(entity_id: &str) -> CodeSymbolReferencesResponse`, `get_file_lens(workspace_id: &str, path: &str, content_hash: &str, parser_version: &str) -> CodeFileLensResponse`. REUSE the existing `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs` HTTP client for all backend code-nav calls (do NOT introduce a second HTTP stack); add the code-nav routes as new methods on that client. Reuse `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/error.rs` for error typing.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/mod.rs` — add `lsp_client: Option<LspClient>`, `code_nav_client: CodeNavClient`, `completion_state: Option<CompletionState>`, `hover_state: Option<HoverState>`. On each `did_change` event (after TextBuffer edit), call `lsp_client.did_change(...)` async. On cursor dwell (500ms without movement), trigger `hover(cursor_position)`. On Ctrl+Space or trigger characters, trigger `completion(cursor_position)`. Wire diagnostics: `lsp_client.on_publish_diagnostics(|diags| panel.push_diagnostics(diags_to_markers(diags)))`.  
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/code_editor/editor_view.rs` — render completion popup as a floating `egui::Window` at the cursor position with a scrollable list of `CompletionItem` (label, kind icon, detail). Arrow keys navigate; Enter accepts; Escape dismisses. Render hover tooltip as a floating `egui::Frame` with markdown-rendered documentation (use `egui_commonmark` if available, otherwise plain text). Render go-to-definition link in the hover (click opens the target file in a new editor pane or scrolls to the line if same file). REUSE the existing command-palette popup pattern from `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_palette.rs` + `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/command_registry.rs` for the keyboard-navigable floating list (selection/keyboard semantics), rather than reinventing list navigation.

Exact crates/functions/APIs to use:
- `tower-lsp = "0.20"` or `lsp-types = "0.95"` + raw stdio JSON-RPC — use `lsp-types` for the type definitions and implement a lightweight JSON-RPC stdio transport rather than the full tower-lsp server stack (tower-lsp is server-side). Use `tokio::process::Command` to spawn the language server.
- `tokio` — already required for async backend calls. LSP requests run on the tokio runtime.
- backend HTTP via `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs` — for CodeNavClient HTTP calls (reuse the existing client; do not add a separate reqwest stack).
- `serde_json` — for LSP JSON-RPC serialization.
- `egui_commonmark` (optional) — for markdown rendering in hover tooltips.
- AccessKit (via `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/*`): `author_id = "code_editor_completion_popup"` `Role::ListBox` on the completion popup; `author_id = "code_editor_completion_item_{n}"` `Role::Option` on each item; `author_id = "code_editor_hover"` `Role::Tooltip` on the hover widget. Register nodes through the existing accessibility registry rather than emitting raw AccessKit.

React reference files:
- `app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.ts` — the entire file is the reference. Ports: `ensureHandshakeCodeIntelligenceProviders` -> `CodeNavClient` methods; `completionKind` -> native equivalent; `refreshHandshakeCodeIntelligenceMarkers` -> `push_diagnostics` call; `codeSymbolStalenessLabel` -> same utility function in Rust.
- `app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.tsx` — displays symbol detail from `getCodeSymbol` and `getCodeFileLens`. The native equivalent is the hover tooltip showing the same data.
- `app/src/lib/api.ts` functions: `getCodeSymbol` -> `GET /knowledge/code/symbols/{entity_id}`, `lookupCodeSymbols` -> `GET /knowledge/code/symbols?workspace_id=...&prefix=...`, `getCodeSymbolReferences` -> `GET /knowledge/code/symbols/{id}/references`, `getCodeFileLens` -> `GET /knowledge/code/files/{path}/lens?workspace_id=...&content_hash=...&parser_version=...`.
- `app/src/harness/mt249_code_intelligence_harness.tsx` — the proof harness for the backend code-nav integration; the native equivalent must pass equivalent real-backend tests.

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. LSP client stdio transport: open `tokio::process::Command::new(server_bin).stdin(Stdio::piped()).stdout(Stdio::piped())`. Spawn a tokio task that reads stdout line-by-line (LSP messages are header+content), parses JSON-RPC responses, and routes them to pending request futures via a `HashMap<RequestId, oneshot::Sender<Value>>`.
2. `completion` request: send `textDocument/completion` with `TextDocumentPositionParams`. Map response `CompletionItem.kind` integers to a `CompletionItemKind` enum using the LSP spec table.
3. `hover` request: send `textDocument/hover`. Extract `contents.value` (MarkupContent) for display.
4. Diagnostics: LSP servers push `textDocument/publishDiagnostics` notifications (not responses to requests). The reader task must detect notification messages (no `id` field) and route them to a notification channel. The `on_publish_diagnostics` callback is called from there.
5. CodeNavClient: all methods are `async fn`; call them from the egui frame using `tokio::task::spawn` and a `std::sync::mpsc::channel` to return results to the next frame (avoid blocking the egui thread).
6. Completion popup rendering: on each frame, if `completion_state.is_some()` and the popup has items, render a `Window::new("completion").fixed_pos(cursor_px_pos).show(ui.ctx(), ...)`. Intercept Arrow Up/Down in the window to navigate `selected_index`. On Enter: insert the selected item's `insert_text` at the cursor using `cursor_set.insert_at_all`.
7. Staleness markers: for CodeNavClient responses, check `staleness.fresh` field (as in `refreshHandshakeCodeIntelligenceMarkers`); if not fresh, push a `GutterMarker::Diagnostic(Warning)` for the affected lines.

Dependencies/unblock conditions: MT-001 (TextBuffer, Highlighter), MT-007 (`push_diagnostics` stub). Requires a real PostgreSQL backend (`handshake_core`) running for proof targets that call the backend code-nav APIs. If backend is unavailable, LSP and CodeNav return empty results gracefully. Cross-WP note: the backend HTTP client this MT reuses (`backend_client.rs`) was delivered by WP-KERNEL-011; this dependency is a reuse pointer, not a depends_on edge.
Risk if missed: No code intelligence means operators cannot use completion, hover, or go-to-definition — the primary productivity features of the VS Code parity goal. Diagnostic markers from LSP are also missing from the gutter.
acceptance_criteria: AC-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native code_nav_client` passes with real PostgreSQL backend — test: `lookup_symbols(workspace_id, 'add', 5)` returns at least one result with `symbol_kind` populated.

AC-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native code_nav_hover` passes with real backend — test: `get_symbol(entity_id)` returns a `CodeSymbolResponse` with `display_name` and `definition.line_start` populated.
AC-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native code_nav_references` passes with real backend — test: `get_references(entity_id)` returns a response with at least one caller or callee.
AC-004: `cargo test -p handshake-native lsp_client_graceful` passes without a language server running — all methods return empty/None without panicking.

AC-005: egui_kittest interactive test: trigger completion on a Rust snippet with backend running, verify completion popup AccessKit node (code_editor_completion_popup ListBox) appears with at least one item.
AC-006: egui_kittest interactive test: hover on identifier 'add' with backend running, verify hover tooltip AccessKit node (code_editor_hover Tooltip) contains 'add' in its text content.
AC-007: `push_diagnostics` is called with at least one marker after a code-nav staleness check — verified via diagnostic gutter dot in screenshot.
AC-008: LSP `publishDiagnostics` notification is received and routed to `push_diagnostics` — test with a mock language server that sends one error diagnostic.
proof_targets: PT-001: `cargo test -p handshake-native code_nav_client` with real PG — GREEN, logged. Backend connection string from env var `HANDSHAKE_TEST_DB_URL`.
PT-002: `cargo test -p handshake-native code_nav_hover` with real PG — GREEN, logged.
PT-003: `cargo test -p handshake-native code_nav_references` with real PG — GREEN, logged.
PT-004: `cargo test -p handshake-native lsp_client_graceful` — GREEN without backend.
PT-005: egui_kittest test `completion_popup_accesskit` with real PG — AccessKit dump shows ListBox.
PT-006: egui_kittest test `hover_tooltip_accesskit` with real PG — AccessKit dump shows Tooltip with symbol name.
PT-007: egui_kittest test `lsp_diagnostics_gutter` with mock LSP — gutter shows diagnostic dot.
implementation_notes: Do not call backend HTTP or spawn LSP tasks from the egui render loop — only poll result channels. Spawn async tasks via `tokio::spawn` in response to input events (key presses, cursor movement).
Completion trigger debounce: only trigger completion if the user has not typed for 200ms. Use a `last_edit_instant: Option<Instant>` in the code editor module and check the elapsed time.
Hover dwell trigger: 500ms dwell at the same cursor position. Use a `hover_dwell: Option<(byte_offset, Instant)>` field.
CodeNavClient result caching: cache `lookup_symbols` results for the same `prefix` string for 2 seconds to avoid hammering the backend on every keypress.
lsp-types crate version: pin to `0.95.*` — this is the version compatible with the LSP 3.17 spec.
Mock LSP server for tests: implement a minimal stdio-based mock in the test module that responds to `initialize` and `textDocument/publishDiagnostics` with canned data.

Reuse the WP-KERNEL-011 backend HTTP client (`src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs`) for all code-nav API calls; this is a cross-WP reuse pointer to WP-011 deliverables, not a same-WP dependency edge.
ports_from_react: app/src/lib/monaco/code_intelligence.ts — complete file is the porting reference: ensureHandshakeCodeIntelligenceProviders (all provider registrations), refreshHandshakeCodeIntelligenceMarkers (staleness markers), codeSymbolStalenessLabel (utility), completionKind (kind mapping), markdownForSymbol (hover content)
app/src/components/CodeSymbolPanel.tsx — CodeSymbolPanel shows the same data as the hover tooltip; port the definitionLabel and fileLensInputs helpers

app/src/lib/api.ts — getCodeSymbol, lookupCodeSymbols, getCodeSymbolReferences, getCodeFileLens: exact HTTP endpoints and query param shapes to replicate in CodeNavClient (reuse src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs as the transport)

risks: RISK-001: LSP server process leaks if the editor panel is closed without sending `shutdown` + `exit`. Mitigate: implement `Drop` for `LspClient` that sends shutdown/exit and waits with a 2-second timeout.
RISK-002: CodeNavClient floods the backend with parallel requests on fast typing. Mitigate: debounce completion trigger (200ms) and cache results (2s TTL).
RISK-003: LSP stdio reader task panics on unexpected server output (e.g. non-JSON debug prints). Mitigate: wrap reader in `catch_unwind` or use `Result`-based parsing that skips malformed lines.

RISK-004: Real-backend tests are skipped when `HANDSHAKE_TEST_DB_URL` is not set — must not fail CI, must emit a clear `cargo test` skip message. Mitigate: use `#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "integration"), ignore)]` attribute gated by a Cargo feature.

RISK-005: Completion popup focus steals input from the editor on the same frame it opens, causing a missed character. Mitigate: render the popup as a non-modal `egui::Window` that does not take keyboard focus; route all keystrokes through the editor first.
minimum_controls: MC-001: LspClient Drop implementation with shutdown is required.
MC-002: Integration tests gated by `integration` Cargo feature to avoid breaking CI without backend.
MC-003: Graceful degradation test (lsp_client_graceful) is required and must pass without backend.
MC-004: Completion debounce constant `COMPLETION_DEBOUNCE_MS = 200` defined in code and commented.
